question: How does the NuScale LTOP system protect the RCS during low-temperature conditions?

nuscale_ch05_0009.title: Overpressure Protection
nuscale_ch05_0009.summary: This section outlines NuScale's compliance with overpressure protection requirements by referencing applicable ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) and Operations and Maintenance (OM) code cases, as mandated by various sections of 10 CFR 50.55a and related Regulatory Guides (RGs). It details how code cases for design, fabrication, construction, preservice, and inservice inspections and testing are selected and verified to meet NRC requirements, with a consolidated list provided in Table 5.2-1.

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Module (NPM) and its reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components comply with relevant U.S. regulations, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria. It details that these components are designed, fabricated, and tested to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) Section III, primarily as Class 1, and references the applicable code editions and regulatory guides used for certification.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the pressurizer and its components in the NuScale reactor, specifying alloys and standards for each part to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments. It references relevant sections of the BPVC (Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code) and lists the applicable material specifications for components such as the baffle plate, heater bundles, cover plate, and bolting.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards related to the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as specific ASME code cases relevant to reactor component materials, quality assurance, and inspection procedures.

nuscale_ch05_0023.title: Reactor Coolant System High-Point Vents
nuscale_ch05_0023.summary: This section describes the testing and inspection requirements for the DHRS (Decay Heat Removal System) components in the NuScale reactor, ensuring compliance with ASME codes for Class 2 components. It details how actuation valves are tested during cold shutdown conditions, outlines the procedures for valve position verification, and specifies that a one-time in-situ test of the DHRS function will be performed on the first installed reactor module as part of the initial test program.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems in the NuScale Power Module, detailing the integration of the reactor, steam generators, pressurizer, safety valves, and associated piping within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV). It outlines the configuration and boundaries of the steam generator system, emphasizing the integral design of the steam generators within the RPV and the separation between the primary and secondary sides.

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer, integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and separated by a baffle plate, regulates Reactor Coolant System (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. Its design ensures stable pressure during various operating conditions, using heaters and spray flow for control, and is sized to handle pressure surges from transients without triggering reactor trips or safety system actuations.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: The steam generators (SGs) in the NuScale Power Module are integrated within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which also houses other key components such as the pressurizer, safety valves, and emergency cooling systems. The SG system includes feedwater and main steam piping, with the RPV serving as the SG shell and forming part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary through the generator tubes and associated welds.

nuscale_ch05_0012.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Leakage Detection
nuscale_ch05_0012.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) does not use traditional components with designed leakage, making it impractical to distinguish between identified and unidentified leakage inside containment using current instrumentation. All leakage into containment is treated as unidentified until the source is found, with leakage rates determined by RCS inventory balance and compared to containment evacuation system (CES) flow rates; the detection systems are reliable and support leak-before-break analyses.

edges.description: Section A provides specific details about the ASME code cases and inservice test program referenced in Section B's general statement of compliance with 10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC requirements.

Section B references Section A by directing the reader to Section 5.2 for material descriptions relevant to the pressurizer, which is part of the RCS overpressure protection system discussed in Section A.

Section A discusses compliance with ASME and NRC regulatory codes for overpressure protection, while Section B lists the specific references (such as ASME and EPRI documents) that support or are cited in Section A's compliance statements.
Section B references Section A by directing the reader to Section 5.2 for material descriptions of the RPV upper shell and upper head, establishing a structural cross-reference.

Section A asserts compliance with ASME BPVC and other regulatory requirements for RCPB integrity, while Section B lists references (including ASME and EPRI documents) that provide the technical standards and guidelines supporting these compliance claims.
Section A references Section B by directing the reader to Section 5.2 for material descriptions relevant to the pressurizer components, which are detailed in Section B.

Section A describes inspection and classification requirements for DHRS components, including actuation valves, which are relevant to overpressure protection as discussed in Section B, which addresses compliance with ASME and NRC requirements for reactor coolant boundary integrity.

Section A describes inspection requirements for DHRS components in accordance with BPVC Section XI, while Section B establishes that RCPB components comply with 10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC Section III, connecting them through their adherence to ASME code requirements for safety-related components.

Section A discusses inspection requirements for DHRS components, while Section B describes the materials and components of the pressurizer, both of which are subsystems within the Reactor Coolant System as referenced in the NuScale FSAR.

Section A describes the components of the NuScale RCS, including those relevant to overpressure protection, while Section B outlines the regulatory and code compliance requirements (e.g., ASME BPVC, 10 CFR 50.55a) that these components must satisfy for overpressure protection.

Section A describes the specific components and subsystems of the NuScale Reactor Coolant System, while Section B establishes that these components comply with regulatory requirements (10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC) for design, fabrication, and inspection.

Section A provides a general overview of the RCS components, including the pressurizer and its subsystems, while Section B specifies detailed material information and references for the pressurizer components mentioned in Section A.

Section A describes the pressurizer's design and function as part of the RCS, which enables rapid pressure communication necessary for overpressure protection, while Section B outlines the regulatory requirements for overpressure protection that the system must comply with.

Section A describes the design and function of the pressurizer as part of the RCS, while Section B states that the RCS components, including the pressurizer, comply with regulatory codes such as ASME BPVC and 10 CFR 50.55a, thus Section A's design details are intended to satisfy Section B's regulatory requirements.

Section A describes the functional design and role of the pressurizer in the RCS, while Section B provides detailed material specifications and references for the pressurizer's components, thus Section B specifies the construction details for the general description in Section A.

Section A details inspection and classification requirements for DHRS components, which are part of the RCS subsystems described in Section B, thereby specifying implementation details for the general system overview in Section B.

Section A provides a general overview of the RCS components, including the pressurizer, while Section B gives specific design details about the pressurizer, which is one of the components listed in Section A.

Section A describes the components of the NuScale reactor coolant system, including those relevant to overpressure protection, while Section B outlines the regulatory requirements and code compliance for overpressure protection of the reactor coolant boundary, thus connecting the physical system design (A) to the regulatory framework (B) that governs its protection functions.

Section A describes the specific components of the NuScale RCS, including the steam generators, while Section B states that these components are designed and constructed in compliance with regulatory codes such as 10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system components, including the pressurizer and its associated subsystems, while Section B specifies detailed material information and references for the pressurizer components mentioned in Section A.

Section A discusses the detection of leakage in the reactor coolant pressure boundary, which is a condition that the overpressure protection system described in Section B is designed to prevent or mitigate, connecting them through the shared goal of maintaining RCS integrity under regulatory requirements.

Section A describes the practical limitations of leakage detection in the RCS, while Section B establishes that the RCS components comply with regulatory codes (10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC), which set the standards for design and inspection, thus Section B provides the regulatory requirements that Section A's discussion of leakage detection must ultimately satisfy.

Section B provides detailed material specifications for pressurizer components that are referenced in Section A's discussion of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, connecting the physical design to the boundary's integrity and leakage considerations.
